source: CIS AWS DATABASE SERVICES BENCHMARK V1.0.0
title: Ensure Security Configurations are Reviewed Regularly

input_row.source: CIS AWS DATABASE SERVICES BENCHMARK V1.0.0
input_row.title: Ensure Security Configurations are Reviewed Regularly
input_row.description: Regularly updating and reviewing the security configuration of your Amazon Keyspaces environment helps ensure that your database is protected against potential vulnerabilities and aligned with your security requirements.
input_row.rationale: Impact: If you are not updating these regularly, your database would most likely become susceptible to a vulnerable attack. Not updating your IAM permission, network, and encryption setting, and controlling audit logging, would lead to the attacker getting into the system which would result in data loss.
input_row.audit: 1. Sign in to the AWS Management Console • Sign in to the AWS Management Console at https://console.aws.amazon.com/ with your AWS account credentials. 2. Open the Amazon Keyspaces Console • Navigate to the service using the Find Services search bar or by directly accessing the console at https://console.aws.amazon.com/keyspaces/. 3. Select the Keyspace • Choose the Keyspace (database) for which you want to update and review the security configuration. • Click on the Keyspace name to access its details page. 4. Review IAM Roles and Permissions • In the Keyspace details page, click on the Configuration tab. • Under the Authentication and access control section, review the IAM roles and permissions associated with the Keyspace. • Ensure that the IAM roles have appropriate permissions and follow the principle of least privilege.  • Review the IAM policies and make any necessary updates to align with your security requirements. 5. Review Network Security • In the Keyspace details page, click on the Configuration tab. • Under the Network & Security section, review the VPC, subnets, security groups, and network ACLs associated with the Keyspace. • Ensure that the VPC and subnet configurations align with your security requirements. • Review the security group rules and network ACL rules to ensure they restrict access to necessary ports, IP ranges, and protocols. • Make any necessary updates to tighten the network security settings. 6. Review Encryption Settings • In the Keyspace details page, click on the Configuration tab. • Under the Encryption section, review the encryption settings for the Keyspace. • Ensure that encryption at rest and in transit are enabled and the appropriate encryption options are chosen. • Review any customer-managed keys used for encryption and verify their configurations. 7. Review Access Control • In the Keyspace details page, click on the Configuration tab. • Under the Authentication and access control section, review the Access Control Lists (ACLs) for the Keyspace. • Ensure the ACLs define appropriate access permissions at the table and row levels. • Review the ACL rules and make any necessary updates to align with your security policies and access requirements. 8. Review Audit Logging • In the Keyspace details page, click on the Configuration tab. • Review the Keyspace's logging configuration under the Logging section. • Ensure the logs are captured and stored in CloudWatch Logs as expected. • Please review the log retention settings and y that they comply with your retention policies. 9. Regularly Monitor Security Bulletins • Stay updated with AWS security bulletins, advisories, and best practices. • Monitor AWS security announcements and subscribe to relevant security notifications.  • Regularly review and apply security patches, updates, and recommended configuration changes for Amazon Keyspaces.
input_row.impact: If you are not updating these regularly, your database would most likely become susceptible to a vulnerable attack. Not updating your IAM permission, network, and encryption setting, and controlling audit logging, would lead to the attacker getting into the system which would result in data loss.

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Manual

**APPROACH DECISION:**

The audit field specifies a range of checks that involve reviewing IAM roles, network security configurations, encryption settings, access control lists, and audit logging configurations within the Amazon Keyspaces console. This involves:

- Human judgment to assess IAM roles and permissions for least privilege adherence.
- Reviewing network configurations, security groups, and NACLs for alignment with security requirements.
- Evaluating encryption settings, both at rest and in transit.
- Ensuring logging configurations meet compliance requirements.
- Staying updated with AWS security bulletins and advisories.

While many of these configurations can be viewed and adjusted via the AWS Management Console, the effectiveness of these settings in applying principles of least privilege, alignment with organizational security requirements, and the appropriateness of the logging and retention settings require human judgment. This makes it challenging to automate purely through API calls because subjective interpretation and policy alignment are involved, which are not directly verifiable with AWS APIs.

**MANUAL STEPS:**

1. **IAM Roles and Permissions:**
   - Sign in to the AWS Management Console and navigate to the Amazon Keyspaces service.
   - Select the Keyspace, navigate to the Configuration tab, and review the IAM roles and permissions.
   - Verify that the IAM roles adhere to the principle of least privilege by examining the permissions granted and ensuring they are necessary and minimal.

2. **Network Security:**
   - Review the VPC, subnets, security groups, and network ACLs under the Network & Security section.
   - Confirm that configurations restrict access to necessary ports, IP ranges, and protocols.
   - Ensure network security settings are updated as required for your security needs.

3. **Encryption Settings:**
   - Check the Encryption section to ensure encryption at rest and in transit are enabled using appropriate options.
   - Verify customer-managed keys configurations if applicable.

4. **Access Control Lists (ACLs):**
   - Review the ACLs for appropriate access permissions for tables and rows, ensuring they align with security policies.

5. **Audit Logging:**
   - Confirm that logging configurations under the Logging section capture logs effectively and comply with retention policies.

6. **Monitoring and Maintenance:**
   - Regularly review AWS security bulletins and advisories.
   - Apply necessary security patches and updates recommended by AWS.

These steps require comprehensive evaluation by a security professional to ensure compliance with organizations' security policies and best practices.
